artifacts.title: STOM FHIR R4 Terminology Server
verified_read_operations: SNOMED CT MRCM allowed attribute lookup
HIRA procedure search and detail lookup
HIRA medication search and detail lookup
HIRA therapeutic-material search and detail lookup

artifacts.title: Local STOM FHIR R4 Terminology Server
verified_read_operations: FHIR ValueSet full read by id
FHIR CodeSystem $validate-code for proposed extension codes
complete LOINC LL Answer List aggregate discovery
individual LOINC LL Answer List dynamic expansion

reference_reconciliation.full_content_comparison_method: canonical search or explicit full read by id

limitations: Live terminology results are provisional mapping candidates and are not clinical rules.
MRCM results are Build-Time semantic constraints and never determine interview priority or safety.
Laterality post-coordination requires versioned 723264001 refset membership and applicable MRCM/normal-form validation.
Do not use the STOM RefsetMemberViewDTO referencedComponentActive field as the sole concept-active check; verify the concept with FHIR CodeSystem $lookup.
The current general API exposes KCD-8 free-text diagnosis search but no general /search/KCD9 endpoint; KCD-9 diagnosis codes can be verified with FHIR CodeSystem $lookup.
KCD-9 morphology search is tumor-morphology-specific and is not a general diagnosis-name search.
HIRA procedure, medication and therapeutic-material results are separate reimbursement catalogs and never replace clinical terminology.
KCD/HIRA claim lookup is reactive to an explicit user- or document-supplied code or catalog/product name and is not routine clinical enrichment.
When both SNOMED CT and KCD/HIRA codings are verified, both remain available with an explicit mapping relation; label similarity alone is insufficient for equivalence.
Only minimal de-identified normalized terms may be sent at Runtime.
The service is optional at Runtime; failure must not block safety assessment or the interview.
Production use requires licensing, privacy, availability and conformance review.
ValueSet create, update and delete are declared by the local server, but this reconciliation publisher authorizes only explicit authenticated creation of absent resources.
Project-owned a-sct, a-loinc, a-local and a-mixed ValueSets must pass local validation and collision checks before publication.

Reference ValueSets are reused when canonical/version and normalized membership match; membership-equivalent resources under another canonical are reported but not substituted; absent references are created unchanged, while extensions are separate a-extended resources.
STOM ValueSet type search returns summary resources without version or compose, so content comparison uses canonical full-resource search or explicit full read by id.

The STOM LOINC Answer List aggregate ValueSet reports version 2.80 while the observed LOINC CodeSystem reports 2.82; both versions are retained and the catalog is unreviewed/research_only until release alignment is resolved.
Official LOINC LL Answer Lists are cataloged by their official canonical and dynamically expanded at Build Time; they are not duplicated as persisted project resources.
